summary: Completed frontend testing of YouTube video looper app. UI loads correctly and most controls are interactive, but critical ReactPlayer integration is broken preventing video playback functionality.

critical_bugs.issue: seekTo method not available - playerRefCurrent4.seekTo is not a function
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks all video seeking, loop functionality, and play controls

critical_bugs.issue: Webpack dev server iframe overlay intercepting click events
critical_bugs.impact: Play/pause button and other controls become unclickable

critical_bugs.issue: YouTube video content not loading in iframe
critical_bugs.impact: No actual video playback, empty black video container

ui_bugs.issue: Recent videos section not appearing after multiple video loads
Success/error toast messages not visible to users

integration_issues.issue: ReactPlayer ref not properly initialized causing method access errors

design_issues.issue: Invalid HTML - span elements inside option tags causing hydration warnings

passed_tests: Page loads with correct dark theme styling
URL input field accepts YouTube URLs
Control deck appears after video load attempt
Loop time input fields accept manual entry
Set start/end time buttons are clickable
Dark theme has good contrast for text readability
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix ReactPlayer ref initialization issue causing 'seekTo is not a function' error. Remove or fix webpack dev server overlay that blocks user interactions. Ensure YouTube iframe properly loads video content. Test and fix localStorage history functionality.

success_percentage.frontend: 35% - UI components load and basic interactions work, but core video functionality is broken due to ReactPlayer issues
